brent.label: Brent Crude $/bbl
brent.change: +49.1% since Feb 27 — surges past $105 after Iran rejects ceasefire and Tangsiri killed; +$9/bbl reversal from Day 26 lows
brent.tooltip: Brent Crude: The international benchmark. Pre-war close: $71.00/bbl (Feb 27). Mar 26 (Thu): $105.85-$106.12, up 3.8-6.4% — sharp reversal after Iran's definitive rejection of 15-point plan and Israel's killing of IRGC Navy Commander Tangsiri. IEA: 'largest supply disruption in the history of the global oil market' — 8 mb/d supply plunge in March, Gulf production cut 10+ mb/d. Brent-WTI spread $12.45 reflects acute waterborne premium. Brent +28% YTD from $82.80. Source: ICE Futures Europe / Fortune / TECHi / IEA.

wti.label: WTI Crude $/bbl
wti.change: +40.8% since Feb 27 — rebounds to $93.61 on Iran rejection + Tangsiri strike; Brent-WTI spread $12.45
wti.tooltip: West Texas Intermediate: The US benchmark. Pre-war close: $66.50/bbl (Feb 27). Mar 26 (Thu): $93.61, up 3.6% from prev. Reversal driven by Iran's definitive ceasefire rejection and Tangsiri killing. Gas at $3.98/gal national average (AAA) — up $1.00 in one month from $2.98. California $5.79-$5.82. Oklahoma cheapest at $3.24-$3.25. Diesel $5.10. USPS 8% fuel surcharge. Source: NYMEX / AAA / AP.

gas.label: US Gas Avg/Gal
gas.change: +$1.00 / +33.6% since Feb 27 — $3.98 AAA avg — up $1.00 in one month — highest since Aug 2022 — CA $5.79-$5.82
gas.tooltip: National average retail gasoline price. Pre-war: $2.98/gal (Feb 27). AAA Mar 26: $3.981/gal (+33.6% since war began). Up $1.00 in one month — highest since August 2022. California $5.79-$5.82 (highest). Oklahoma $3.24-$3.25 (cheapest). Diesel $5.10. USPS imposed 8% fuel surcharge. AP-NORC: 45% extremely/very concerned about gas affordability (up from 30% pre-war). 59% say war 'gone too far.' Source: AAA / AP-NORC.

sp500.label: S&P 500
sp500.change: -329 pts / -4.8% — stocks fall as Iran hardens position; oil surge weighs on Wall Street; mixed signals
sp500.tooltip: S&P 500 index. Pre-war close: 6,878.88 (Feb 27). Mar 26 (Thu): ~6,550-6,592 — mixed signals; AP headline: 'stocks fall and oil prices rise as uncertainty about war with Iran weighs on Wall Street.' Iran's definitive ceasefire rejection and Tangsiri killing drove oil higher and equities lower. S&P -5.8% YTD. VIX averaging 24.3 in March (vs 16.1 Feb). Defense: RTX +22.1%, LMT +19.4% MTD. Energy: XOM +16.4% MTD. Airlines/cruise battered. Source: NYSE / AP / Bloomberg.

daily_cost.label: Est. Daily US Cost
daily_cost.note: ~$1.0-1.5B/day — 9,000+ targets — 9,000+ sorties — 82nd Airborne en route — Tangsiri killed — power plant deadline ~Mar 28 — $200B supplemental
daily_cost.tooltip: Estimated daily operational cost. Day 27: ~$1.0-1.5B/day. Israel kills IRGC Navy Commander Tangsiri in Bandar Abbas. 82nd Airborne (~2,000 paratroopers) en route to CENTCOM — Kharg Island seizure suspected. CENTCOM: 9,000+ targets, 9,000+ combat sorties. Two-thirds of Iran's missile/drone production destroyed. Pentagon $200B supplemental before Congress. Penn Wharton: $40-95B direct military, $50-210B economic. Replenishment $3.1B+ (~$758M/day). Source: Pentagon / CSIS / CENTCOM / Penn Wharton.

total_cost.label: Est. Total Cost (27 Days)
total_cost.note: Pentagon $11.3B Day 6 + CSIS $16.5B Day 12 + ~$1-1.5B/day ongoing — ~$31.5-32.1B — $200B supplemental proposed
total_cost.tooltip: Cumulative estimated cost since Feb 28. Pentagon: $11.3B through Day 6. CSIS: $16.5B through Day 12. Extrapolated through Day 27: ~$31.5-32.1B. Pentagon $200B supplemental before Congress. Speaker Johnson: 'probably' in reconciliation bill. Trump expects war 4-6 weeks. Penn Wharton: $40-95B direct military, $50-210B economic. Replenishment: $3.1B+ (~$758M/day). Source: Pentagon / CSIS / The Hill / Penn Wharton.

targets_struck.label: Targets Struck
targets_struck.note: CENTCOM: 9,000+ US — 9,000+ combat sorties — ~333 targets/day — IDF: 'wave of extensive strikes' on Isfahan arms factories — 2/3 of missile/drone production destroyed
targets_struck.tooltip: Total targets struck by US and Israeli forces since Feb 28. CENTCOM (Mar 26): 9,000+ US targets, 9,000+ combat sorties (~333 targets/day over 27 days). Two-thirds of Iran's missile and drone production facilities destroyed. Israel launches 'wave of extensive strikes' on central Isfahan targeting remaining missile/drone production. IDF 'speeding up targeting over next 48 hours' focusing on arms factories. Combined US/Israeli extrapolated ~28,000+ through Day 27. Source: CENTCOM / IDF / NPR / Al Jazeera.

us_kia.label: US Military Deaths
us_kia.note: 13 KIA total — Sgt. Pennington (Glendale, KY) latest — 290 WIA (255 RTD, 10 seriously wounded)
us_kia.tooltip: US military deaths. 13 KIA total including Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, 26, of Glendale, Kentucky — latest announced. 290 WIA (255 returned to duty, 10 seriously wounded). 82nd Airborne (~2,000 paratroopers) en route to CENTCOM — Maj. Gen. Tegtmeier commanding. Source: Department of War / CENTCOM / Stars and Stripes.

us_wia.label: US Service Members WIA
us_wia.note: 290 total — 255 returned to duty — 10 seriously wounded — 50,000+ deployed + 2,000 82nd ABN en route
us_wia.tooltip: US service members wounded/injured across all theaters since Feb 28. 290 WIA (255 returned to duty, 10 seriously wounded). 50,000+ US troops in CENTCOM AOR plus ~2,000 82nd Airborne paratroopers en route. Source: Department of War / CENTCOM / Pentagon.

iranian_killed.label: Iranian Killed (est.)
iranian_killed.note: HRANA: 3,230+ documented (1,464 civ + mil) — Red Crescent: 1,400+ killed / 18,000+ wounded — Al Jazeera: ~1,937 — Health Min: 210+ children
iranian_killed.tooltip: Iranian casualties — significant discrepancies across sources. HRANA: 1,464 confirmed civilian fatalities (217 children) plus military — 3,230+ total documented by Mar 21. Iranian Red Crescent: 1,400+ killed, 18,000+ civilians injured, 204 children. Al Jazeera tracker: ~1,937. Tehran alone: 503 killed, 6,848 injured. Health Ministry: 300 health facilities damaged, 210+ children. IRGC Navy Commander Tangsiri killed Mar 26. Navy lost 43+ warships, 80% air defenses destroyed. Source: HRANA / Red Crescent / Al Jazeera / Health Ministry.

lebanese_killed.label: Lebanese Killed
lebanese_killed.note: 1,001+ killed — 2,584 wounded — ~1M displaced (~20% of pop) — Hezbollah man killed in Nahariya — IDF soldier killed in Lebanon gunfight
lebanese_killed.tooltip: Lebanese casualties from Israeli strikes and Hezbollah operations since March 2. 1,001+ killed, 2,584 wounded. ~1 million displaced (~20% of population). Day 27: Hezbollah rocket kills man in Nahariya, IDF soldier killed in gunfight in southern Lebanon. Hezbollah chief Qassem rejected truce: 'negotiations under fire amounts to imposing surrender.' Israel expanding buffer zone toward Litani River (~30km). Hezbollah/Amal ministers boycotted Lebanese cabinet over Iran ambassador expulsion. Source: Lebanon Health Ministry / Times of Israel / Al Jazeera.

displaced.label: Displaced Persons
displaced.note: 4.1M internally displaced + 117K refugees — Iran 3.2M — Lebanon 1M+ — 24,600 Afghans returned — UNHCR $454.2M appeal
displaced.tooltip: People displaced from their homes across all fronts. UNHCR: 4.1 million internally displaced, 117,000 seeking refuge abroad. Iran: up to 3.2M (UNHCR). Lebanon: 1M+ (~20% of pop). 24,600 Afghans returned from Iran. UNHCR published emergency Inter-Agency Refugee Response Plan Mar 26 requiring $454.2 million. 20,000-40,000 seafarers stranded. 3,000+ vessels idled. IFRC $51.5M emergency appeal for 5M people. Source: UNHCR / OCHA / IFRC / IMO.

flights_cancelled.label: Flights Cancelled
flights_cancelled.note: 52,000+ flights — 3,000+ vessels stranded — 40,000+ sailors — 21+ vessel attacks — 7+ seafarer deaths
flights_cancelled.tooltip: Commercial flights cancelled due to airspace closures: 52,000+ since Feb 28 (Travel Tourister/Cirium). 3,000+ vessels stranded in Middle East (IMO via NPR Mar 21). 40,000+ seafarers stranded. ~150 ships anchored outside Hormuz, ~170 containerships (450,000 TEU) trapped inside Gulf. 15,000+ cruise passengers on 6+ ships. 21+ maritime attacks, 7+ seafarer fatalities. JMIC/UKMTO: CRITICAL. Insurance at 5% of vessel value. Source: IMO / IATA / Bloomberg / UKMTO.

children_killed.label: Children Killed
children_killed.note: Iran 210+ (Health Min) / HRANA 217 / Red Crescent 204 + Lebanon 118+ + Israel 4 + Kuwait 1 — Minab school: 168 killed (100+ schoolchildren)
children_killed.tooltip: Children killed in the conflict. Iran Health Ministry: 210+ children killed. HRANA: 217 children confirmed. Red Crescent: 204 children. Amnesty verified Minab school: 168 killed (100+ schoolchildren aged 7-12) — US Tomahawk fragments confirmed — outdated DIA intel. UN investigation initiated Mar 17, ongoing. Lebanon: 118+ children (UNICEF). Israel: 4. Kuwait: 1. UNICEF: 2,100+ children killed or injured across region. Source: UNICEF / Amnesty / HRANA / Red Crescent / Iran Health Min.
